# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].

Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Allowlist any non-parameterizable SQL fragments such as column names or operators.
# Category: sql_injection

class ReportsController < ApplicationController
  def generate_report
    query = params[:query]
    if query.present?
      # Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection
      sanitized_query = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.quote(query)
      results = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute(sanitized_query)
      render json: { results: results }
    else
      render json: { error: "No query provided" }
    end
  end
end
